title: DAO ACK not implemented for reliable downward routing
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl

RFC 6550 Section 6.5 defines DAO-ACK messages for reliable DAO delivery. The K flag in DAO indicates whether an ACK is requested.

Current implementation:
- DAO parsing reads ack_requested flag (`dao->ack_requested`)
- DAO building sets ack_requested = false
- No DAO-ACK message type is defined or handled

Impact: Without DAO-ACK support, nodes cannot confirm their routes were installed at the root, potentially leading to:
1. Silent route installation failures
2. Nodes transmitting data before routes are established
3. No retry mechanism for lost DAOs

This is documented as a limitation but should be addressed for production use.

Suggested fix:
1. Define DAO-ACK message structure and codec
2. Add DAO-ACK handler in dao_manager
3. Implement DAO retransmission with exponential backoff until ACK received
close_reason: Implemented DAO-ACK codec, build handler in dao_manager, enabled ack_requested by default for reliable routing. Retransmission with backoff left for follow-up epic as it requires additional state and timer integration.
